{"id":213,"date":"2026-06-25T10:15:41","date_gmt":"2026-06-25T10:15:41","guid":{"rendered":"http:\/\/beesayatv.test\/?post_type=trail&p=213"},"modified":"2026-07-23T11:12:43","modified_gmt":"2026-07-23T11:12:43","slug":"malingin-rock","status":"publish","type":"trail","link":"http:\/\/beesayatv.test\/trails\/malingin-rock\/","title":{"rendered":"Malingin Rock | Cotcot River, Liloan | 2026 GPX"},"content":{"rendered":"\n

Malingin Rock is a short out-and-back hike in Mulao, Liloan, leading to the Cotcot River. The route descends through farmland and rural mountain scenery before reaching the river, then returns along the same trail with a short but steeper uphill climb back to the jump-off point. The trail is easy to follow and suitable for first-time solo hikers.<\/p>\n\n\n

Trail Snapshot<\/h3>
EASY<\/div>View others \u2192<\/a><\/div>
\n
\ud83d\udcc5 Documented<\/span>May 2026<\/strong><\/div>
\ud83d\udccf Distance<\/span>1.5 km (Out and Back)<\/strong><\/div>
\u26f0 Elevation Gain<\/span>106 m<\/strong><\/div>
\ud83d\udd52 Duration<\/span>2 hrs<\/strong><\/div>
\ud83d\udca7 Water<\/span>At least 1 liter<\/strong><\/div>
\ud83c\udf33 Shade<\/span>Limited<\/strong><\/div>\n
\ud83d\udc63 Surface<\/span>100% trail<\/strong><\/div>
\ud83d\uddfa\ufe0f GPX<\/span>Wikiloc<\/a><\/strong><\/div> <\/div><\/div>\n\n\n\n

Access<\/h2>\n\n\n\n

The documented hike began near Mulao Barangay Hall, which was reached via a concrete road. Motorcycle parking was available along the roadside, and no parking fee was observed during the hike. Private vehicles could access the jump-off point, while public transportation was not available. Habal-habal services were available.<\/p>\n\n\n\n

From Liloan proper, the documented drive to Mulao Barangay Hall was approximately 11 km. Some residents along the access road raised pigs, and occasional strong odors were noted in parts of the area.<\/p>\n\n\n

Why this trail is rated Easy<\/h3>
\u2713 Easy-to-follow trail<\/div>
\u2713 Straightforward navigation<\/div>
\u2713 Short hiking distance<\/div>
\u2713 No technical climbing required<\/div>
\u2713 Suitable for first-time solo hikers<\/div><\/div>

On this trail:<\/span> river crossing<\/a> \u2022<\/span> rock formation<\/a><\/p><\/div><\/div><\/div>\n\n\n\n

Route Description<\/h2>\n\n\n\n

The route follows a mostly downhill trail through farmland toward the Cotcot River. Navigation is straightforward, and the short distance makes the approach relatively easy.<\/p>\n\n\n\n

After reaching the river and Malingin Rock, the return follows the same route, with a short but more tiring uphill climb back to the jump-off point.<\/p>\n\n\n\n\n\n\n

Trail Conditions<\/h2>\n\n\n\n

Conditions during the documented hike were sunny and dry. Most of the trail was shaded, except for the river section. Stores were available only at the jump-off point.<\/p>\n\n\n\n

Mobile signal was strong near the start but became weaker near the river. At least 1 liter of water was recommended. No other hikers were encountered during the hike, and insect activity was minimal.<\/p>\n\n\n\n

Additional Notes<\/h2>\n\n\n\n

Due to Typhoon Odette and Typhoon Tino, Malingin Rock was observed to have shifted approximately 11 meters downstream from its previous location. During the documented hike, it was much closer to the trail exit leading to the river.<\/p>\n\n\n\n
